candidate.role: Required non-index sleeve candidate to diversify retained QM5_20009 NDX Sleeve A
candidate.family: Same-bar confirmed-pivot liquidity raid plus displacement/MSS plus three-candle FVG
candidate.portfolio_unit: One inseparable two-symbol, two-session candidate; no symbol, session or side may be selected or dropped after outcomes.

selection_freeze.primary_symbol_reason: XAUUSD is named in the primary source title, is an approved-card target, and supplies a metal sleeve independent of the retained NDX index sleeve.
selection_freeze.transport_symbol_reason: EURUSD is an approved-card target and tests whether the exact structural engine transports from metal to liquid FX without parameter changes.
selection_freeze.timeframe_reason: M15 is the interval embedded in the primary publication chart. The Pine is chart-timeframe generic, while the later card's M5 declaration is not evidenced by the publication snapshot; source-chart M15 is frozen without testing alternatives.
selection_freeze.session_diversification: The source sessions are 07:00-10:00 and 12:30-16:00 America/New_York. They do not overlap retained NDX Sleeve A's 10:00-11:00 America/New_York entry window.
selection_freeze.no_parameter_search: swingLen=5 and reward/risk=2.0 are the only tested values. No alternate timeframe, symbol subset, session subset, stop, target, spread or news mode is permitted in this DEV decision.

known_before_contract: APPROVED card, SPEC, committed EA and exact open-source Pine rules.
TradingView page metadata and publication-chart symbol, M15 interval and America/New_York timezone.
Timestamp prefixes only for input availability and M5-to-M15 completeness; no selected OHLC field was parsed.
Venue symbol metadata, one live 2026 bid/ask spread observation per symbol, exact commission sources, news calendar hash and DST implementation.
No QM5_10729 trade list, report, strategy result or pre-2023 performance metric was inspected.
prohibited_before_both_contract_commit_a: Parsing open, high, low, close or volume from either selected market file.
Hashing a whole market file or any row tail at or after the future fence.
Running the strategy engine on market outcomes.
Changing any rule, gate or input after any outcome is observed.
outcome_protection.release_protocol: After outcome-blind independent review and an exact-path commit, report the commit and SHA256 of this contract. An explicit release naming that frozen hash is required before an outcome parser or auditor may run.
outcome_protection.failure_rule: Report NO_MERIT without tuning, optimization, alternative variants or post-hoc subset selection. Any future candidate requires a new source-rationale freeze and untouched OOS data.

data_contract.input_time_basis: Darwinex broker wall-clock encoded as timezone-naive Unix seconds
data_contract.file_drift_rule: Before outcome access, require exact file length, last-write timestamp and timestamp-only availability above. Any mismatch aborts for a new outcome-blind freeze; do not parse OHLC under this contract.
data_contract.future_fence: For each raw row, parse only the timestamp prefix before the first comma. Break immediately when timestamp >= 2023-01-01 broker-naive epoch. Never split, convert, hash or otherwise inspect that row's OHLC/volume tail. Report future_ohlc_parsed=false or reject.
data_contract.selected_slice_hash: After explicit outcome release only, SHA256 the canonical raw rows with 2018-01-01 <= timestamp < 2023-01-01. Never hash the whole market file. Persist the per-symbol slice hashes in every result.
data_contract.validation: Require strictly increasing unique timestamps, finite positive OHLC, low <= min(open,close) <= max(open,close) <= high, nonnegative volume and exact expected header. Reject rather than repair.

eurusd_resampling.required_source_rows: Exactly one M5 row at bucket+0, bucket+300 and bucket+600 seconds. Any duplicate or missing slot makes the M15 bucket ineligible; do not interpolate.
eurusd_resampling.ohlcv: open=first open; high=max three highs; low=min three lows; close=last close; tick_volume=sum three tick volumes.
eurusd_resampling.causality: The derived M15 bar becomes available only at bucket_open+900 seconds.

data_contract.causal_bar_eligibility: Never inspect later timestamps to admit or suppress an earlier signal. Each currently completed direct or derived M15 bar is evaluated using only state and data available through its close.
data_contract.forced_flat_boundary_integrity: If a position remains open for a scheduled session/Friday flat, the exact final M15 bar ending at that boundary must exist. If it does not, the entire run is INTEGRITY_FAIL with no merit verdict; do not retroactively suppress the earlier trade, substitute a stale/future price or invent swap.
data_contract.missing_bar_rule: Do not synthesize bars. An incomplete EURUSD derived bucket emits no M15 bar. Pivot state advances on each available completed M15 bar exactly as a chart would; timestamp gaps do not reset state and do not trigger a future-completeness entry filter.

clock_and_session_contract.source_timezone_basis: Pine time(timeframe.period, session) supplies no timezone and therefore defaults to the publication chart symbol exchange timezone recorded in the source evidence.
clock_and_session_contract.broker_dst: QM_DSTAware US-DST convention: Darwinex broker is UTC+2 outside US DST and UTC+3 during US DST; November ambiguity prefers standard offset.
clock_and_session_contract.constant_mapping_reason: America/New_York is UTC-5 outside US DST and UTC-4 during US DST, so the bound broker clock is exactly New York plus seven hours in both regimes.

sessions.name: LONDON_LABEL
sessions.forced_flat: 17:00 broker closing tick of the 16:45 bar

sessions.name: NEW_YORK_LABEL
sessions.forced_flat: 23:00 broker closing tick of the 22:45 bar

clock_and_session_contract.session_test_time: Match the M15 bar opening time, exactly as Pine time() does.
clock_and_session_contract.entry_time: Signal and market entry occur on the confirmed M15 closing tick. The close time must be strictly before that source session's end and, on Friday, strictly before 21:00 broker.
clock_and_session_contract.session_label: Every trade is attributed to its signal bar's unique non-overlapping source session.

state_order_on_each_completed_bar: First evaluate ta.pivothigh/ta.pivotlow candidates centered five bars earlier using the now-complete eleven-bar window.
If confirmed, update lastHigh and/or lastLow before evaluating the current bar's signal, matching Pine statement order.
Then evaluate both direction expressions on the current bar. Valid OHLC makes bullish and bearish FVG expressions mutually exclusive.
Pivot state updates on every available M15 bar, including bars outside trade sessions, news blackouts and while a position is open.
source_faithful_signal_engine.pivot_high: Candidate high at i-5 must be strictly greater than each of the five highs on its left and each of the five highs on its right. Equality with any neighbor emits no pivot.
source_faithful_signal_engine.pivot_low: Candidate low at i-5 must be strictly less than each of the five lows on its left and each of the five lows on its right. Equality with any neighbor emits no pivot.
long_same_bar: lastLow is available after the current bar's pivot update.
current low < lastLow and current close > lastLow.
current close > immediately prior M15 bar high.
current low > high two M15 bars earlier.
current bar-open is inside a source session and all execution/news gates pass.
short_same_bar: lastHigh is available after the current bar's pivot update.
current high > lastHigh and current close < lastHigh.
current close < immediately prior M15 bar low.
current high < low two M15 bars earlier.
current bar-open is inside a source session and all execution/news gates pass.
source_faithful_signal_engine.strictness: All structural inequalities are strict, exactly as the Pine operators. No epsilon, point tolerance, displacement-size threshold, trend bias, order block, FVG retest, delayed FVG arm or parameterized lookback is added.
signal_funnel_required: run-level integrity failure for a missing required flat-boundary bar

execution_overlay.bridge_limit: This is an exact port of Pine v1 entry primitives and ordering on the publication-chart M15 context. Fixed-risk sizing, bid/ask execution, bound per-symbol currency news compliance, Friday close and card-required session-end flat are explicit V5 research overlays because Pine does not implement them.
execution_overlay.position_state: At most one open position per symbol. No pending orders and no pyramiding. A signal while that symbol has a position is counted but ignored; it is neither queued nor used to reverse.
execution_overlay.cross_symbol_concurrency: XAUUSD.DWX and EURUSD.DWX may each hold one position simultaneously, so maximum initial portfolio risk is 2R = USD 2000.
execution_overlay.spread_prices: Input OHLC is bid. For a fixed scenario spread s, ask open/high/low/close equals bid open/high/low/close plus s.

entry.long: Buy at signal-bar ask close = bid close + s.
entry.signal_bar_exit: No SL, TP or session exit is evaluated before the entry closing tick. Protective exits begin on the next M15 bar.

stop_and_target.long: stop is signal-bar bid low; actual initial risk distance = ask entry - bid stop; bid-side TP = ask entry + 2.0 * risk distance.
stop_and_target.short: stop is signal-bar ask high = bid high + s; actual initial risk distance = ask stop - bid entry; ask-side TP = bid entry - 2.0 * risk distance.
stop_and_target.invalid: Reject entry if risk distance <= 0 or target price <= 0. Levels never trail, move to breakeven or scale out.

risk.lot_formula: 1000 / (initial risk distance * USD value per price unit per lot)

future_bar_exit_order: At bar open, a gap through SL exits at the adverse bid/ask open. A gap through TP exits at the fixed TP, with no favorable limit improvement.
Then test the full bid/ask range. If both SL and TP are reachable, resolve SL first. Otherwise fill the reachable fixed level.
If still open on a session's final M15 bar, process SL/TP first and then force-flat at that bar's closing bid for long or closing ask for short.
On Friday the 20:45-21:00 broker bar is the final permitted holding bar: process SL/TP first, then force-flat at its closing bid/ask. The later source session remainder is entry-ineligible.
execution_overlay.session_flat_rationale: The APPROVED card explicitly requires session-end flat, whereas Pine v1 has no session exit or swap model. The flat overlay is frozen before outcomes, excludes final-bar entries that would otherwise open exactly at the flat boundary, and guarantees zero overnight/weekend swap exposure.
execution_overlay.friday_flat: Close at Friday 21:00 broker closing tick if the card session has not already ended; no signal whose closing tick is at or after that boundary may enter.
execution_overlay.swap: Exactly zero by construction because every accepted trade is closed within its originating source session and never crosses broker rollover.
execution_overlay.slippage: Zero explicit slippage in center and adverse scenarios; spread and exact venue commission are the only execution-cost axes. This remains a DEV screen, not execution-parity certification.
execution_overlay.portfolio_exit_order: For pooled closed-balance drawdown, exits sharing a timestamp are processed loss-first, then win, with deterministic symbol/session/entry-time tie keys. Daily and yearly totals remain order-invariant.
execution_overlay.conservative_equity_path: Start from USD 100000. At every M15 close, deduct entry/exit commissions and mark each surviving long at bid close and short at ask close. Also calculate a within-bar adverse portfolio equity bound by summing each concurrently open trade's contract-valid worst value: gap/SL execution value when stopped, otherwise bid low for long or ask high for short. Treat those per-symbol adverse values as simultaneous even when true tick ordering is unknown. Entry bars contribute closing spread plus entry commission but no pre-entry range. Track drawdown from the running adjusted-equity high-water mark and each broker day's minimum versus its flat day-start balance.

spread_contract.selection_rule: No averaging or best-case selection; CENTER determines all main gates and ADVERSE is mandatory robustness.
spread_contract.evidence_limit: The center is one read-only live DXZ observation and adverse is exactly 2x center. Registry spread parity remains OPEN; passing does not certify live or FTMO spread/slippage parity.

external_cost_contract.native_or_source_commission: Do not apply Pine's illustrative commission_value=0.01 percent and do not apply native tester commission. Apply the venue schedule below exactly once.
external_cost_contract.rounding: Each entry and exit deal-side commission is independently rounded half-up to integer USD cents. Sum integer cents before converting to USD or R.
external_cost_contract.EURUSD.DWX: round_cent_half_up(max(2.50 USD, 2.50 EUR * deal_price_in_USD_per_EUR) * lots) per deal side
external_cost_contract.XAUUSD.DWX: round_cent_half_up(deal_price_USD_per_oz * 100 oz * lots * 0.000025) per deal side
external_cost_contract.cost_burden: sum external commission R / sum positive gross R; fail if positive gross R denominator is zero.
external_cost_contract.source_priority: EURUSD uses max(DXZ EUR2.50 converted at each deal price, FTMO USD2.50) per side from the bound auditor/venue registry. XAU uses the exact conservative 0.0025 percent notional per side from the tester Groups and venue registry.

news_contract.global_fail_closed_tokens: An empty event currency or ALL affects both symbols, matching QM_NewsEventAffectsSymbol. For XAUUSD, a literal XAU token would also match the normalized base but is not expected in the bound macro calendar.
news_contract.blackout: Inclusive [event broker time - 30 minutes, event broker time + 30 minutes]. Reject an entry if any instant of the closed signal-bar interval [bar open, bar close] intersects any blackout.
news_contract.event_conversion: Use the exact bound QM_DSTAware US-DST broker conversion; November ambiguity prefers standard offset.
news_contract.open_position: Do not force-close an existing position for news. Fixed SL/TP/session/Friday rules continue.
news_contract.state_during_blackout: Pivot state and raw signal funnel continue to update; only new entry is blocked.

deviations_and_ambiguities_frozen_before.observation: The card/SPEC say M5, but the source publication chart is M15. This contract uses M15 once, with no timeframe comparison.

The card/SPEC/EA copy 07:00-10:00 and 12:30-16:00 as broker HHMM. Exact Pine defaults them to the chart exchange timezone, America/New_York; this contract maps them to broker 14:00-17:00 and 19:30-23:00.
Pine requires sweep, reclaim, MSS and FVG on the same current bar. The committed EA arms sweep+MSS and accepts a later FVG for six bars. This contract preserves the exact same-bar source expression.

The committed EA accepts equal neighbor extrema. TradingView's official Operators page (https://www.tradingview.com/pine-script-docs/language/operators/) says ta.pivothigh(5,5) requires five lower highs on both sides; the mirrored pivot-low rule requires higher lows. This contract therefore requires strict uniqueness and records ties as no pivot.

Pine v1 has no session flat. The APPROVED card requires it, and exact swaps are otherwise unavailable. This contract force-flats at each originating session end and excludes the final session bar from entry. The committed EA instead waits until the later New York end for all positions.
Pine pyramiding=0 can model opposite strategy.entry reversal semantics. The approved card requires one active position; this contract ignores all signals while open and never reverses.

Pine uses one chart price and illustrative 0.01 percent commission. This contract models bid/ask explicitly, preserves true executable 2R from actual entry to actual stop, and substitutes exact venue commission.

No frozen EURUSD.DWX_M15 CSV exists at the export path. EURUSD M15 is deterministically aggregated from bound M5 rows; incomplete buckets emit no bar and are never interpolated. No later session-completeness lookahead suppresses an earlier signal; a missing required flat-boundary bar fails the run.
Historical/current spread parity is OPEN. Fixed center/adverse snapshots are preregistered robustness assumptions, not certification.
The card frontmatter is APPROVED while its prose Pipeline-Verlauf says PENDING. Frontmatter is treated as authoritative.

familywise_merit_gates.family_definition: Four preregistered symbol-by-session cells: EURUSD/LONDON_LABEL, EURUSD/NEW_YORK_LABEL, XAUUSD/LONDON_LABEL and XAUUSD/NEW_YORK_LABEL. All are reported; none may be selected post hoc.
familywise_merit_gates.center_adjusted_net: Strictly >0 pooled, for each symbol, each session, each side and each of the four symbol-session cells.

center_adjusted_profit_factor.definition: sum positive adjusted R / abs(sum negative adjusted R); no losses means infinity; no wins fails.
center_adjusted_profit_factor.pooled_dynamic_floor: z=2.497705474412374 (two-sided alpha 0.05 Bonferroni across four preregistered cells); u=z/sqrt(N pooled center fills); d=u/sqrt(1+u^2); floor=max(1.20,(1+d)/(1-d))

familywise_merit_gates.candidate_verdict: FAMILYWISE_MERIT only if every center and adverse gate passes. Otherwise NO_FAMILYWISE_MERIT. There is no partial symbol/session sleeve verdict.
familywise_merit_gates.portfolio_limit: FAMILYWISE_MERIT makes this candidate eligible for a separate preregistered joint portfolio test with retained NDX Sleeve A; it does not itself prove FTMO suitability or authorize deployment.

required_reporting: Integrity, file metadata, selected-slice hashes, raw/derived timestamp counts and future_ohlc_parsed globally and per symbol.
Complete signal funnel per scenario, symbol, session and side, including every rejection counter.
Trade ledger with deterministic IDs, signal/entry/exit times and prices, direction, session, stop, target, lots, gross USD/R, each integer-cent deal commission, adjusted USD/R and exit reason.
Center and adverse performance pooled and by symbol, session, side, symbol-session cell, year and symbol-year.
Gross and adjusted net, PF, expectancy, win rate, closed-balance DD, conservative bar-equity DD, worst closed/equity broker day, cost burden and concurrency.
Top-two winner share, leave-best-trade-out, leave-best-year-out, same-bar conflicts and exit-reason counts.
Every gate with exact observed value and pass/fail plus the single candidate verdict.
Two independent full executions with byte-identical canonical JSON, each refusing any timestamp at or after 2023 before its OHLC tail is parsed.
